message:
fix(types): strengthen cancel reason in Outcome::join

Outcome::join() previously was purely left-biased on equal severity,
meaning Cancelled.join(Cancelled) always kept the left reason. Now
when both outcomes are Cancelled, the stronger CancelReason is retained
via CancelReason::strengthen(). This ensures that e.g., a Timeout
cancellation is not silently replaced by a weaker Explicit cancellation
when joining concurrent outcomes.

Also updated join_outcomes() free function to delegate to Outcome::join()
instead of duplicating the severity comparison. Doc comments clarified.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
